passingResults.code:
import { Tabs } from '@tale-ui/react/tabs';
import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text';
import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column';

export function SettingsPage() {
  return (
    <Column gap="l">
      <Text variant="heading" as="h1">Settings</Text>
      <Tabs.Root defaultSelectedKey="account">
        <Tabs.List variant="pills">
          <Tabs.Tab id="account">Account</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="security">Security</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Tab id="notifications">Notifications</Tabs.Tab>
          <Tabs.Indicator />
        </Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Panel id="account">
          <Column gap="s">
            <Text variant="heading" as="h2">Account</Text>
            <Text color="muted">Manage your account information, profile details, and preferences.</Text>
          </Column>
        </Tabs.Panel>
        <Tabs.Panel id="security">
          <Column gap="s">
            <Text variant="heading" as="h2">Security</Text>
            <Text color="muted">Update your password, enable two-factor authentication, and review active sessions.</Text>
          </Column>
        </Tabs.Panel>
        <Tabs.Panel id="notifications">
          <Column gap="s">
            <Text variant="heading" as="h2">Notifications</Text>
            <Text color="muted">Choose how and when you receive email, push, and in-app notifications.</Text>
          </Column>
        </Tabs.Panel>
      </Tabs.Root>
    </Column>
  );
}
passingResults.prompt: Show a settings page with pill-style tabs: Account, Security, and Notifications, each with a content panel describing that section.
